creator: martin@lispworks.com
text:
(In reply to Fedor Uporov from comment #11)

Later comments show that there is no /dev/ada0p5 (or in fact any /dev/ada0??) so I don't think that can be the problem.

The "Invalid fstype" message is misleading and is caused by a bug in mount that occurs if the special device name doesn't exist (see bug #274600).
